title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 388.625 - Railroad police, powers of
text:
388.625.  Railroad police, powers of. — Railroad policemen, while engaged in the pursuit of their purposes in regard to violations of the law which occurred on railroad property, shall have in every county and city in this state all law enforcement powers which county and city peace officers have except for the serving and execution of civil process, provided, however, that a railroad policeman shall not apply for or serve search warrants.
(L. 1971 H.B. 232 § 6)
